mnemonics: Many Xylophones Play Songs - Multiplexer (MUX) selects one input (X) from many to output (Y) based on selector (S)
Adders Always Calculate Everything Right - A stands for Adder, C for Carry, E for Everything, R for Result
keyPoints: A multiplexer is a combinational circuit that selects one of several input lines and directs it to a single output line
A half-adder adds two bits and produces a sum and carry output
A full-adder adds three bits (including carry-in) and produces sum and carry-out outputs
HDL (Hardware Description Language) models describe digital circuits using text-based code rather than schematic diagrams
commonMistakes: Confusing selector inputs with data inputs in multiplexer designs
Forgetting to account for carry-in when designing full adders
Mixing behavioral and structural HDL modeling styles incorrectly
Not properly handling bit widths in HDL adder implementations
